item.title: Strait Talk
item.description:
Strait Talk

By Michael Every of Rabobank

Strait Talk

Despite many false dawns, markets remain upbeat on prospects for peace between the United States and Iran. Secretary of State Rubio indicated that the US side had thought it would have something to announce on Sunday night, or “maybe today” given that the Sunday deadline has now passed, and it is actually Tuesday. Striking a more cautionary tone, Iranian President Pezeshkian said of prospects tha
item.content:encoded:
Strait Talk

By Michael Every of Rabobank

Strait Talk

Despite many false dawns, markets remain upbeat on prospects for peace between the United States and Iran. Secretary of State Rubio indicated that the US side had thought it would have something to announce on Sunday night, or “maybe today” given that the Sunday deadline has now passed, and it is actually Tuesday. Striking a more cautionary tone, Iranian President Pezeshkian said of prospects tha
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: DNA Databases, Data Breaches, and the Search for “Nonhuman” Genetics
item.description: Consumer ancestry databases have become powerful investigative tools—raising questions not only about privacy and law enforcement access, but also about speculative claims involving anomalous or even extraterrestrial DNA. Consumer DNA databases were once marketed as little more than a way to explore ancestry and family history. Today, they sit at the center of growing debates […] The post DN
item.content:encoded: Consumer ancestry databases have become powerful investigative tools—raising questions not only about privacy and law enforcement access, but also about speculative claims involving anomalous or even extraterrestrial DNA. Consumer DNA databases were once marketed as little more than a way to explore ancestry and family history. Today, they sit at the center of growing debates […] The post DN
item.dc:creator: Casey North

item.title: Nearly 1.2 Billion People Live With Mental Disorders Globally: Study
item.description:
Nearly 1.2 Billion People Live With Mental Disorders Globally: Study

The study assessed the prevalence of 12 types of mental disorders across 204 nations and territories between 1990 and 2023. Types of disorders assessed in the study included bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder, major depressive disorder, and anxiety disorders. All mental di
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 1.2 Billion People Live With Mental Disorders Globally: Study

The study assessed the prevalence of 12 types of mental disorders across 204 nations and territories between 1990 and 2023. Types of disorders assessed in the study included bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder, major depressive disorder, and anxiety disorders. All mental di
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: How does "explicit consent" apply to video on-premises?
item.description: To set the stage, I'm talking specifically about video surveillance inside a physical building, under the rules set forth by GDPR (and similar) and as interpreted by regulatory authorities. Under those rules, consent must be "freely given", which means companies cannot force you to consent to non-essential data processing as a condition for using their service (known as tyi
item.content:encoded: To set the stage, I'm talking specifically about video surveillance inside a physical building, under the rules set forth by GDPR (and similar) and as interpreted by regulatory authorities. Under those rules, consent must be "freely given", which means companies cannot force you to consent to non-essential data processing as a condition for using their service (known as tyi
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Crypto Whales sit on the most auditable wealth ever created and can't always get a bank account.
item.description:
Certain crypto whales are in a peculiar position. They hold wealth that is, by every technical measure, more auditable than any traditional asset: every transaction, every wallet, every transfer sits on a public ledger any compliance officer can read. Yet banks sometimes treat incoming fiat from a crypto exchange as radioactive.

The actual friction point for crypto w
item.content:encoded:
Certain crypto whales are in a peculiar position. They hold wealth that is, by every technical measure, more auditable than any traditional asset: every transaction, every wallet, every transfer sits on a public ledger any compliance officer can read. Yet banks sometimes treat incoming fiat from a crypto exchange as radioactive.

The actual friction point for crypto w
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: BP Shares Plunge After Chairman Abruptly Removed Over Serious Governance Concerns
item.description:
BP Shares Plunge After Chairman Abruptly Removed Over Serious Governance Concerns

Shares of BP PLC, formerly British Petroleum, dropped in London trading after the oil and gas major said its board had removed Chairman Albert Manifold with immediate effect over "serious concerns" tied to "important governance standards, oversight, and conduct."

The abrupt ouster adds yet another layer of instability to BP's turnaround effort; more importantly, it rai
item.content:encoded:
BP Shares Plunge After Chairman Abruptly Removed Over Serious Governance Concerns

Shares of BP PLC, formerly British Petroleum, dropped in London trading after the oil and gas major said its board had removed Chairman Albert Manifold with immediate effect over "serious concerns" tied to "important governance standards, oversight, and conduct."

The abrupt ouster adds yet another layer of instability to BP's turnaround effort; more importantly, it rai
item.dc:creator: Elena Vasquez

item.title: Cloud storage alternatives to google cloud
item.description:
I’m looking to change my google cloud storage case I hate google and I hate that storage like them and OneDrive are not actually private or sell your data, I heard cryptomator can help encrypt the files when putting them into the cloud service but $40 is a lot considering I want to have my files uploaded from iOS, Android, windows and Linux.
Is there an alternative tha
item.content:encoded:
I’m looking to change my google cloud storage case I hate google and I hate that storage like them and OneDrive are not actually private or sell your data, I heard cryptomator can help encrypt the files when putting them into the cloud service but $40 is a lot considering I want to have my files uploaded from iOS, Android, windows and Linux.
Is there an alternative tha
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Daily Crypto Discussion - May 25, 2026 (GMT+0)
item.description:
Welcome to the Daily Crypto Discussion thread. Please read the disclaimer and rules before participating.

Disclaimer:

Consider all information posted here with several liberal heaps of salt, and always cross check any information you may read on this thread with known sources. Any trade information posted in this open thread may be
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to the Daily Crypto Discussion thread. Please read the disclaimer and rules before participating.

Disclaimer:

Consider all information posted here with several liberal heaps of salt, and always cross check any information you may read on this thread with known sources. Any trade information posted in this open thread may be
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: Cars Without Eye Tracking
item.description:
With new cars having the ability to track our eye movements and breathing patterns (to check if we are “tired” or “inebriated”), clearly this is a major privacy issue.

Does anyone know which newer cars DON’T have this? Anything 2024 and newer?

I need a new (at least new to me) car but don’t want my car recording my face 24/7

sub
item.content:encoded:
With new cars having the ability to track our eye movements and breathing patterns (to check if we are “tired” or “inebriated”), clearly this is a major privacy issue.

Does anyone know which newer cars DON’T have this? Anything 2024 and newer?

I need a new (at least new to me) car but don’t want my car recording my face 24/7

sub
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: China Moves To Shut Down Offshore Stock-Trading Channels Used By Mainland Investors
item.description:
China Moves To Shut Down Offshore Stock-Trading Channels Used By Mainland Investors

The agencies involved are the CSRC, Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, Ministry of Public Security, People’s Bank of China, State Administration for Market Regulation, National Financial Regulatory Administration, Cyberspace Administration of China, and State Administration
item.content:encoded:

item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: 'Weeks Inside Highly Fortified Bunkers': Report Details Painfully Slow Communication Within Iran's Leadership
item.description:
'Weeks Inside Highly Fortified Bunkers': Report Details Painfully Slow Communication Within Iran's Leadership

According to a Sunday CBS News report citing US officials, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is still in hiding in a secret location with extremely limited communication to the outside world. Driven underground by a pervasive fear within Tehran's remaining leadership structure following relentless US and Israeli military strikes, the Supreme L
item.content:encoded:
'Weeks Inside Highly Fortified Bunkers': Report Details Painfully Slow Communication Within Iran's Leadership

According to a Sunday CBS News report citing US officials, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is still in hiding in a secret location with extremely limited communication to the outside world. Driven underground by a pervasive fear within Tehran's remaining leadership structure following relentless US and Israeli military strikes, the Supreme L
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Corrections Vs Bears: How The Fed Rewired The Market
item.description:
Corrections Vs Bears: How The Fed Rewired The Market

After three decades of watching market cycles play out from both sides of the trade, I’ve come to a simple conclusion: Wall Street’s love of simple rules is one of the most dangerous aspects of investing. When stocks fall 10%, it’s just a “correction.” However, if they decline 20%, it’s a “bear market.” Simpl
item.content:encoded:
Corrections Vs Bears: How The Fed Rewired The Market

After three decades of watching market cycles play out from both sides of the trade, I’ve come to a simple conclusion: Wall Street’s love of simple rules is one of the most dangerous aspects of investing. When stocks fall 10%, it’s just a “correction.” However, if they decline 20%, it’s a “bear market.” Simpl
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: Huawei Touts Sanctions-Busting Chip Breakthrough, SMIC Shares Erupt
item.description:
Huawei Touts Sanctions-Busting Chip Breakthrough, SMIC Shares Erupt

Semiconductor Manufacturing International soared to a record high in China after Huawei unveiled what it described as a breakthrough pathway for advanced semiconductor production at the IEEE ISCAS conference, without relying on the West's most advanced chipmaking equipment.

Huawei's semiconductor chief, He Tingbo, told the audience earlier today that the company has developed a "New Semiconductor P
item.content:encoded:
Huawei Touts Sanctions-Busting Chip Breakthrough, SMIC Shares Erupt

Semiconductor Manufacturing International soared to a record high in China after Huawei unveiled what it described as a breakthrough pathway for advanced semiconductor production at the IEEE ISCAS conference, without relying on the West's most advanced chipmaking equipment.

Huawei's semiconductor chief, He Tingbo, told the audience earlier today that the company has developed a "New Semiconductor P
item.dc:creator: Elena Vasquez

item.title: Could realistic face masks actually hide your face from age verification?
item.description: I heard about the data breach from Roblox's and Discord's ai (persona) and I'm thinking of using my face mask. I heard that they could still sell your data and recognize you even when using a mask according to google ai. I'm not really sure how that would work since i don't see any part of the face being visible and although google's ai isn't accurate, they're not wrong mos
item.content:encoded: I heard about the data breach from Roblox's and Discord's ai (persona) and I'm thinking of using my face mask. I heard that they could still sell your data and recognize you even when using a mask according to google ai. I'm not really sure how that would work since i don't see any part of the face being visible and although google's ai isn't accurate, they're not wrong mos
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: OnlyFans "Hack" Hoax Likely Used To Push Malware-Laced Leak Checkers
item.description:
OnlyFans "Hack" Hoax Likely Used To Push Malware-Laced Leak Checkers

A cyber threat actor advertised a purported database of 340 million OnlyFans-linked user records on a well-known cybercrime forum, asking for 0.313 BTC, or roughly $76,000, according to U.K.-based cybersecurity news site on X this past holiday weekend, garnering millions of views from several accounts, which were described as nothing m
item.content:encoded:
OnlyFans "Hack" Hoax Likely Used To Push Malware-Laced Leak Checkers

A cyber threat actor advertised a purported database of 340 million OnlyFans-linked user records on a well-known cybercrime forum, asking for 0.313 BTC, or roughly $76,000, according to U.K.-based cybersecurity news site on X this past holiday weekend, garnering millions of views from several accounts, which were described as nothing m
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: What the current least-privacy-UNfriendly AI chatbot?
item.description:
Assume someone must choose 1 AI chatbot.

Paid subscription.

Who is most-preferable (or least-unpreferable) provider for such a service?

I realize this may divide opinion, and there probably are different schools of thought, but I may need one, and would like to use whatever opSec is possible, which begins with the initial selection.

Sorry if
item.content:encoded:
Assume someone must choose 1 AI chatbot.

Paid subscription.

Who is most-preferable (or least-unpreferable) provider for such a service?

I realize this may divide opinion, and there probably are different schools of thought, but I may need one, and would like to use whatever opSec is possible, which begins with the initial selection.

Sorry if
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: 70% Of All Crypto 'Wrench Attacks' Happen In France: Report
item.description:
70% Of All Crypto 'Wrench Attacks' Happen In France: Report

About 70% of all wrench attacks, physical attacks against crypto holders and their families, carried out in an attempt to steal digital assets, occur in France, according to Bitcoin journalist Joe Nakamoto.

There have been
item.content:encoded:
70% Of All Crypto 'Wrench Attacks' Happen In France: Report

About 70% of all wrench attacks, physical attacks against crypto holders and their families, carried out in an attempt to steal digital assets, occur in France, according to Bitcoin journalist Joe Nakamoto.

There have been
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: The Email Privacy Act would require the government to get a warrant to access emails and other electronic information
item.description:
The Email Privacy Act was introduced on May 22, 2026 by Suzan DelBene (D-WA) and co sponsored by Warren Davidson (R-OH).

This bill is very similar to the recently introduced submitted by
item.content:encoded:
The Email Privacy Act was introduced on May 22, 2026 by Suzan DelBene (D-WA) and co sponsored by Warren Davidson (R-OH).

This bill is very similar to the recently introduced submitted by
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: White House approves $9B for spy agencies to catch up on AI
item.description:
WASHINGTON — The White House has approved a secret $9 billion request to acquire the cutting-edge computer chips that America’s spy agencies need to tap into the full capabilities of the latest artificial intelligence models, according to current and former U.S. officials.

submitted by
item.content:encoded:
WASHINGTON — The White House has approved a secret $9 billion request to acquire the cutting-edge computer chips that America’s spy agencies need to tap into the full capabilities of the latest artificial intelligence models, according to current and former U.S. officials.

submitted by
item.dc:creator: Marcus Webb

item.title: Upgrading to new phone
item.description: Is it bad practice to keep 2fa and password manager on old android phone or should I just delete them since I'm not using it anymore? The phone has a relatively strong password (17 digit alpha-numeric combo). From what I understand all of those exploits that 3 letter agencies use to get into phones only work when the phone is in an AFU state whereas my old phone would just
item.content:encoded: Is it bad practice to keep 2fa and password manager on old android phone or should I just delete them since I'm not using it anymore? The phone has a relatively strong password (17 digit alpha-numeric combo). From what I understand all of those exploits that 3 letter agencies use to get into phones only work when the phone is in an AFU state whereas my old phone would just
item.dc:creator: Jordan Calloway

item.title: Futures, Global Stocks Soar To All Time High, Oil Plunges On Endless "Iran Deal" Drumbeat
item.description:
Futures, Global Stocks Soar To All Time High, Oil Plunges On Endless "Iran Deal" Drumbeat

US equity futures jumped and global stocks rose to record highs as crude oil fell after officials signaled - once again - that the US was nearing a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows. The dollar weakened while precious metals and crypto bounced from Friday's drop. While the US and Iran closed in on a deal, Trump said he won’t “rush” into an agr
item.content:encoded:
Futures, Global Stocks Soar To All Time High, Oil Plunges On Endless "Iran Deal" Drumbeat

US equity futures jumped and global stocks rose to record highs as crude oil fell after officials signaled - once again - that the US was nearing a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows. The dollar weakened while precious metals and crypto bounced from Friday's drop. While the US and Iran closed in on a deal, Trump said he won’t “rush” into an agr
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.content:encoded: Debt Remembered And Debt Ignored
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: At Last Minute, SEC Suddenly Delays Plan To Allow Crypto Versions Of US Stocks
item.description:
At Last Minute, SEC Suddenly Delays Plan To Allow Crypto Versions Of US Stocks

The SEC, under Chair Paul Atkins,

The SEC was reportedly leaning toward permitting third-party tokens — digital representations of stocks like
item.content:encoded:
At Last Minute, SEC Suddenly Delays Plan To Allow Crypto Versions Of US Stocks

The SEC, under Chair Paul Atkins,

The SEC was reportedly leaning toward permitting third-party tokens — digital representations of stocks like
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: The Pentagon's Disclosure Theater: Why 162 "Released" UFO Files Remain Effectively Hidden
item.description: The Pentagon released classified UAP documents without search functions or metadata, creating the appearance of transparency while blocking systematic public access. An investigation into controlled disclosure architecture.
item.content:encoded: The Pentagon released classified UAP documents without search functions or metadata, creating the appearance of transparency while blocking systematic public access. An investigation into controlled disclosure architecture.
item.dc:creator: Casey North

item.title: The Declassification Illusion: How the Pentagon Releases UFO Files While Hiding Them
item.description: Trump's UAP disclosure order unleashed 162 Pentagon documents—with no search function, no indexing, and no metadata. An investigation into the architecture of controlled transparency.
item.content:encoded: Trump's UAP disclosure order unleashed 162 Pentagon documents—with no search function, no indexing, and no metadata. An investigation into the architecture of controlled transparency.
item.dc:creator: Casey North

item.title: The Pentagon's Disclosure Illusion: 162 Files, Zero Access
item.description: The Pentagon released declassified UAP documents without search functions or metadata, suggesting controlled transparency rather than genuine disclosure. Analysis reveals a systematic pattern obscuring unexplained phenomena data.
item.content:encoded: The Pentagon released declassified UAP documents without search functions or metadata, suggesting controlled transparency rather than genuine disclosure. Analysis reveals a systematic pattern obscuring unexplained phenomena data.
item.dc:creator: Casey North

item.title: The Pentagon's UAP Release Paradox: 162 Files, Zero Search Function
item.description: The Pentagon released 162 declassified UAP documents without indexing or metadata. An analysis of this rollout reveals a pattern of controlled disclosure that limits public access to unexplained phenomena data.
item.content:encoded: The Pentagon released 162 declassified UAP documents without indexing or metadata. An analysis of this rollout reveals a pattern of controlled disclosure that limits public access to unexplained phenomena data.
item.dc:creator: Casey North

item.title: "The World Is Losing Trust": Foreign Investment In Germany Plunges To Lowest Level Since 2009
item.description:
"The World Is Losing Trust": Foreign Investment In Germany Plunges To Lowest Level Since 2009

An analysis by the auditing and consulting firm EY, reported by the said 245,500 industrial jobs had been lost in Germany since 2019, before the coronavirus crisis.

Volkswagen has become one of the clearest examples of the problems facing German
item.content:encoded:
"The World Is Losing Trust": Foreign Investment In Germany Plunges To Lowest Level Since 2009

An analysis by the auditing and consulting firm EY, reported by the said 245,500 industrial jobs had been lost in Germany since 2019, before the coronavirus crisis.

Volkswagen has become one of the clearest examples of the problems facing German
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: Zcash hits near 7 year high against Bitcoin.
item.description:
Zcash is probs the only mature coin to hit a many year high against BTC. Is this a case of the limits of Bitcoin, or a theme of privacy emerging in crypto.

submitted by
item.content:encoded:
Zcash is probs the only mature coin to hit a many year high against BTC. Is this a case of the limits of Bitcoin, or a theme of privacy emerging in crypto.

submitted by

item.title: WW2 N@ZI secret bunker factory ST. Georgen MT
item.description: I did extensive digging into the 3rd Reich which led down a very dark rabbit hole I posted a video about this facility but never turned it into a technical document. I believe the victims of this camp should be remembered because for what ever reason this camp is hidden in plain sight but considering Himmler basically spent a lot of tim
item.content:encoded: I did extensive digging into the 3rd Reich which led down a very dark rabbit hole I posted a video about this facility but never turned it into a technical document. I believe the victims of this camp should be remembered because for what ever reason this camp is hidden in plain sight but considering Himmler basically spent a lot of tim
item.dc:creator: Diana Reeves

item.title: The Inherited IRA 10-Year Rule Is Fully Enforced In 2026 - What Beneficiaries Need To Do Now
item.description:
The Inherited IRA 10-Year Rule Is Fully Enforced In 2026 - What Beneficiaries Need To Do Now

The Internal Revenue Service waived penalties for missed withdrawals from 2021 through 2024 while the rules
item.content:encoded:
